commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainers should decide whether to require the focused regression test before merge because the patch changes provider-config interpretation.; Cleared: The diff only normalizes an existing provider config field before catalog planning; it adds no dependencies, install hooks, downloaded code, permissions, or new secret access paths. (sha=5a5c8bf8d6a4627772975ba8a02ca57be8074e6f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR strips blank `models.providers.*.baseUrl` values before provider discovery and catalog merging so partial provider config does not clobber bundled provider defaults.

PR surface: Source +20. Total +20 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. The current source path shows blank explicit `baseUrl` can flow into discovery/merge and then fail `filterWritableProviders`; the PR body also includes before/after output from the production `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` plus `resolveModelAsync` path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers should decide whether to require the focused regression test before merge because the patch changes provider-config interpretation.

target.title: fix(models): keep bundled provider catalog when configured base URL is blank (#91270)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91270. On the embedded runtime, Google Gemini models stop resolving with `model_not_found`:

```
Unknown model: google/gemini-2.5-flash. Found agents.defaults.models["google/gemini-2.5-flash"],
but no matching models.providers["google"].models[] entry.
```

even though the bundled Google catalog and forward-compat resolver know `gemini-2.5-flash` and `gemini-flash-latest`. The reporter hit this with `models.mode: "merge"` and a config edited by a plugin; their workaround was to add explicit `models.providers.google.models[]` rows.

## Root cause

When a `models.providers.<id>` entry exists with a blank `baseUrl: ""` (as written by a plugin or a partial hand edit), that empty string flows into models.json planning:

- It is spread into the provider-discovery config (`buildPluginCatalogConfig`) and the catalog merge, where it overrides the bundled transport base URL, so the discovered Google catalog ends up with `baseUrl: ""`.
- `isWritableProviderConfig` then treats the provider as non-writable (`baseUrl?.trim() && apiKey` is false), so `filterWritableProviders` drops the whole Google provider from the generated registry, including its bundled catalog and forward-compat templates.
- With no Google rows in the registry, the direct lookup (`gemini-2.5-flash`) and the forward-compat clone (`gemini-flash-latest`, which clones a `gemini-2.5-flash` template) both fail, so resolution falls through to the `model_not_found` registration hint.

A blank `baseUrl` should mean "use the provider default", not "erase the bundled catalog".

## Fix

`resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps` strips blank provider `baseUrl` values from the explicit config before they reach discovery and the merge, so the bundled catalog base URL survives and the provider stays writable. The change is provider-agnostic.

## Verification

Local (Node 22.19.0):

- `pnpm tsgo:core` exit 0; `oxfmt --check src/agents/models-config.plan.ts` clean; type-aware oxlint on the changed file clean.
- Behavior reproduced and fixed through the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` + `resolveModelAsync` path (throwaway harness, not committed): before the fix `google/gemini-2.5-flash` and `google/gemini-flash-latest` fail with the reported error; after the fix both resolve.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Embedded runtime fails to resolve `google/gemini-2.5-flash` / `google/gemini-flash-latest` (`model_not_found`) when a `models.providers.google` entry exists with a blank `baseUrl` and no/partial `models[]` (merge mode, plugin-edited config).
- Real environment tested: Local checkout at `origin/main` 5c5391836b1, Node 22.19, `GEMINI_API_KEY` set; drove the production `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` then `resolveModelAsync("google", id, ...)` decision path for the reporter-shaped config across three variants.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: generate models.json then resolve `google/gemini-2.5-flash` and `google/gemini-flash-latest` for: (C1) no explicit google provider, (C2) explicit google with model rows, (C3) explicit google `{ api, baseUrl: "" }` with no model rows.
- Evidence after fix (real resolver decision path):
  ```
  # before fix
  C3 google/gemini-2.5-flash    => ERR: Unknown model: google/gemini-2.5-flash. Found agents.defaults.models[...] but no matching models.providers["google"].models[] entry ...
  C3 google/gemini-flash-latest => ERR: Unknown model: google/gemini-flash-latest. ...
  # after fix
  C3 google/gemini-2.5-flash    => OK api=google-generative-ai
  C3 google/gemini-flash-latest => OK api=google-generative-ai
  ```
  C1 and C2 resolved both ids before and after the fix (no regression).
- Observed result after fix: all three configs resolve both Gemini ids on the embedded runtime.
- What was not tested: a live request against the Google Gemini API (resolution only, no network call); non-Google providers were not exercised live, though the fix is provider-agnostic.

## Related (not duplicates)

- #85042 (blank/missing google config misroutes the Gemini key to OpenAI) and #89192 (incomplete `model_not_found` remediation message) are adjacent issues in the same provider-config cluster. Keeping the bundled catalog when `baseUrl` is blank helps that cluster but does not fully cover their separate asks.
target.author: yetval
